methods.description: This method describes the process of analyzing biomarkers using Gas Chromatography-Mass Spectrometry (GCMS) to identify and quantify lipid compounds. It is used to differentiate the source inputs for organic matter and reconstruct paleoenvironmental conditions.

steps.description: One microliter of the sample was injected in splitless mode using helium as a carrier gas; the injector temperature was set at 300°C.
steps.searchable_summary: Inject sample into GCMS in splitless mode with helium carrier gas

steps.description: The initial temperature was at 35°C with 1 min hold time followed by ramping from 35 to 130°C at 20°C/min; ramping from 130 to 320°C was at 4°C/min with 15 min hold time.
The mass detector was operated in full scan mode with a mass range (m/z) of 50-600 Da under electron impact ionization.
steps.searchable_summary: Operate mass detector in full scan mode

steps.description: The individual lipid compounds were identified based on their retention times using a standard mixture of fatty acid methyl esters from Sigma Aldrich and matching the mass spectra with the NIST and AOCS Lipid Library.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify lipid compounds using retention times and mass spectra

steps.description: Quantification of fatty acids was based on the GCMS response and comparison of peak areas with known quantities of deuterated eicosanoic acid methyl ester added to the samples before GCMS analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Quantify fatty acids using GCMS response and peak area comparison

methods.description: This method outlines the collection of drip water and moonmilk samples from the cave for subsequent analysis. It is used to gather samples for lipid extraction and stable isotope analysis.

steps.description: We collected drip water samples in acid rinsed glass bottles from four locations on the way to the cave's inner chamber.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect drip water samples in acid rinsed glass bottles

steps.description: We made multiple trips to the cave within 1 week to transport speleothem samples and large volumes of water needed for lipid extraction.
steps.searchable_summary: Transport speleothem samples and water for lipid extraction

steps.description: We also collected two moonmilk samples (MM-1 and MM-2) from the inner chamber and along the passageway leading into the Goldfish pond.
The moonmilk slurry was collected with disposable gloves and forceps and then placed into sterilized 250 ml Schott Duran bottles in an icebox and transported to the lab.
steps.searchable_summary: Store moonmilk slurry in sterilized bottles and transport to lab

steps.description: We stored the moonmilk samples at 4°C in the laboratory until analysis.
